primary_focus: Suspense, async data fetching patterns, error boundaries, and concurrent rendering

suspense_signals.correct: Wrap async-data-dependent components in <Suspense fallback={...}>. Use React 19's `use()` hook to read promises inside components, or create a Suspense-compatible data resource (promise-based cache pattern). Each independent data section (profile, posts, followers) has its own Suspense boundary so they load progressively.
suspense_signals.acceptable_alternative: Using `use()` hook (React 19) to unwrap promises directly inside a component wrapped in Suspense.
suspense_signals.anti_pattern_tier1: No Suspense at all — using useEffect + useState + isLoading for all data fetching, resulting in manual loading state management across every component
suspense_signals.anti_pattern_tier2: A single Suspense boundary wrapping the entire profile view — defeats progressive loading since all three sections wait for the slowest one

suspense_signals.signal: Granular Suspense boundaries for progressive loading
suspense_signals.correct: Separate <Suspense> boundaries for Profile Info, Posts, and Followers sections. Each has its own fallback skeleton. When posts take 1000ms and followers take 700ms, followers appear first.
suspense_signals.anti_pattern: One Suspense boundary around all three sections — everything waits for the slowest fetch

suspense_signals.correct: <Suspense fallback={<PostsSkeleton />}> with purpose-built skeleton components that match the shape of the loaded content
suspense_signals.anti_pattern: Generic spinner or 'Loading...' text as fallback instead of content-shaped skeletons

suspense_signals.correct: When navigating from one user profile to another (via follower click), wrap the navigation state update in startTransition so the old profile stays visible until the new data is ready (Requirement 15)
suspense_signals.anti_pattern: Navigation immediately shows loading skeletons, flashing the UI to a blank state before new data arrives

error_handling_signals.signal: Error boundaries for per-section error handling
error_handling_signals.correct: Each section (profile, posts, followers) wrapped in an error boundary that catches fetch failures and shows a retry button. One section failing does not affect others (Requirement 14).
error_handling_signals.anti_pattern_tier1: No error boundaries — errors crash the entire app or are caught by a single top-level boundary
error_handling_signals.anti_pattern_tier2: Error handling via try/catch in useEffect with error state — works but misses the Suspense+ErrorBoundary pattern that React 19 encourages

error_handling_signals.signal: Retry mechanism resets the error boundary
error_handling_signals.correct: Retry button resets the error boundary (via key prop change or resetErrorBoundary callback) and re-triggers the data fetch
error_handling_signals.anti_pattern: Retry calls the fetch function directly and manages error/data state manually, bypassing the boundary

derived_values_that_must_not_be_state.name: postPreview
derived_values_that_must_not_be_state.trigger: Requirement 11: first 100 characters of post body

derived_values_that_must_not_be_state.name: followerInitials
derived_values_that_must_not_be_state.correct: Already provided by the API in the Follower type — just render it
derived_values_that_must_not_be_state.anti_pattern: Recompute or store in separate state

legitimate_effect_uses.purpose: NONE expected if using Suspense correctly
legitimate_effect_uses.note: With Suspense + use(), data fetching does not require useEffect at all. The presence of useEffect for data fetching is a signal that the developer is using the pre-Suspense pattern.

legitimate_effect_uses.purpose: If NOT using Suspense: useEffect for data fetching
legitimate_effect_uses.expected_pattern: useEffect with cleanup/abort for each fetch, proper loading/error state management. This works but is the older pattern and should score lower on Effect Hygiene.

performance_signals.why: Requirement 15: navigating between profiles should not flash to loading state. startTransition keeps the old UI visible until new data is ready.
Requirement 13: returning to user list should not re-fetch. The data/promise should be stored outside the component or in a stable ref.

accessibility_checkpoints: Loading skeletons have aria-busy='true' or role='status'
User list items are keyboard-navigable (buttons or links)
Error messages are announced to screen readers (role='alert' or aria-live)
Back button is a proper <button> element

event_handler_patterns.interaction: Clicking a user to navigate to profile
event_handler_patterns.correct: onClick handler that updates navigation state, possibly wrapped in startTransition
event_handler_patterns.anti_pattern: useEffect watching selectedUserId to trigger data fetching

event_handler_patterns.correct: onClick handler that resets the error boundary (e.g., incrementing a key), causing Suspense to re-trigger the fetch
event_handler_patterns.anti_pattern: onClick that manually calls the fetch function and sets state
